original_abstract: This research explores the impact of the discussion method on student learning outcomes within the subject of Islamic Religious Education. Conducted at Madrasah Aliyah Negeri (MAN) 1 Tangerang, the study addresses the need for instructional strategies that foster critical thinking, self-confidence, and mutual respect among students. Utilizing a quantitative correlational approach, data were gathered from a sample of 38 eleventh-grade students through observations, Likert-scale questionnaires to measure the implementation of the discussion method (Variable X), and objective tests to assess learning outcomes (Variable Y). Statistical analysis using the Pearson Product Moment formula yielded a correlation coefficient ($r$) of 0.34, exceeding the critical table value of 0.32 at a 5% significance level. Furthermore, the $t$-test result ($t_{count} = 2.32 > t_{table} = 2.03$) confirms a significant positive influence. The findings indicate that the discussion method accounts for approximately 11% of the variance in student performance relative to materials on commendable and despicable morals. The study concludes that the discussion method is an effective pedagogical tool for enhancing student engagement and academic achievement in Islamic Education, providing a viable alternative to traditional teacher-centered approaches.
humanized_abstract: This study aimed to examine the effect of discussion method used as teaching tool on student’s learning outcome in Islamic Religious Education subject at Madrasah Aliyah Negeri (MAN) 1 Tangerang. According to the Ministry of Religious Affairs, discussion method is supposed to foster the student’s creativity, confidence, and humanity in the classroom. The current study employed quantitative correlational method. The data were collected from 38 eleven-grade students of MAN 1 Tangerang by the mean of test, observation, and Likert scale on the implementation of discussion method (Variable X) and student’s learning outcome (Variable Y). The analysis of the data used Pearson Product Moment showed a correlation coefficient (r) amounting to 0.34, exceeding the critical table value of 0.32 at 5% level. The result of t-test was tcount = 2.32 > ttable = 2.03. It means that the discussion method used in teaching and learning process at MAN 1 Tangerang significantly and positively influences the student’s learning outcome relating to commendable and despicable morals with variation of 11%. Thus, the discussion method used in Islamic Religious Education can be implemented as a model of learning activity to attract students’ participation and improve the students’ achievement.
